g.text: Figure 8: The Subtle Safety Bug — Raft Log Safety
Phase 1: Term 2 — Entry Replicated but NOT Committed
Phase 2: Term 3 — Different Entry at Index 2 Committed
Entry {term:2, idx:2, cmd:x} on 2/5 nodes — NOT majority, NOT committed
Entry {term:3, idx:2, cmd:y} on 3/5 nodes — MAJORITY — COMMITTED!
Cluster State — S5 CRASHED, S1 LEADER

text.tspan: If S1 overwrites, COMMITTED DATA LOST!
Previous-term entries are committed INDIRECTLY when a current-term entry is committed after them.
When S1 (term 4) commits entry {term:4, idx:3}, entries before it become committed.
But S1 must first overwrite S3/S4's {term:3, idx:2} entry via AppendEntries consistency check.
The AppendEntries consistency check forces S3/S4 to truncate their {term:3, idx:2} entry
and replace it with S1's entry. Only THEN can S1 commit. This ensures:
1. No committed entry is ever overwritten (leader completeness)
2. Old {term:3, cmd:y} is discarded BEFORE commit proceeds
3. Linearizability maintained — clients never see both 'x' and 'y' at index 2
// Replicates entry at index 2 to S1, S2
// Only on S1, S2 (2 of 5 nodes)
// Replicates entry at index 2 to S1, S2
// Only on S1, S2 (2 of 5 nodes)
// S5 appends NEW entry at index 2
// S5 appends NEW entry at index 2
// S1 elected in term 4 (log is "up to date")
// BUG: If S1 commits entry at index 3
// SAFETY VIOLATION if we commit term 2 entry
// S1 elected in term 4 (log is "up to date")
// BUG: If S1 commits entry at index 3
// SAFETY VIOLATION if we commit term 2 entry
